matches.match_rationale: The core pain is an inability to produce credible enrollment forecasts under varying growth assumptions. Time series forecasting directly replaces the flat-trend extrapolation with models that learn seasonality, feeder-school pipelines, and historical enrollment cycles. For a multi-site operator, site-level models can be aggregated with shared trend components to give network-wide projections.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A forecasting layer ingesting historical enrollment data per campus that outputs 1-3 year enrollment projections under baseline, conservative, and optimistic scenarios — fed into an existing spreadsheet or BI tool.

matches.match_rationale: Sensitivity analysis around drivers like fee changes, feeder-school size, or marketing spend requires estimating how a continuous input change propagates to enrollment numbers. Regression models (including gradient-boosted trees or linear models) can quantify elasticity of demand to price, geography, and economic indicators — exactly the 'what if fees increase 10%' question leadership cannot currently answer.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An enrollment demand model that accepts slider inputs (fee level, marketing budget, new campus location) and outputs estimated intake change with confidence intervals — surfaced as a simple scenario calculator UI.

matches.match_rationale: When leadership wants to test the impact of multiple policy levers simultaneously (price, marketing spend, campus expansion), the combinatorial space is too large for manual what-if grids. Bayesian optimization and multi-armed bandit framing can guide which scenario combinations are worth modelling deeply — prioritizing the highest-information scenarios rather than exhaustive grid search.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A scenario prioritization tool that takes the operator's strategic levers and automatically recommends the most informative combinations to model next, reducing analyst time spent on low-signal scenario variants.

matches.match_rationale: Once scenario models produce numeric outputs, the gap is translating them into board-ready narratives explaining implications, risks, and recommended actions. LLM-generated commentary that wraps quantitative scenario results in plain-language board memos directly addresses the reporting burden and ensures leadership can act on forecasts rather than interpret raw tables.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A report generation layer that takes scenario model outputs (enrollment projections, sensitivity tables) and auto-drafts a 1-2 page executive summary with scenario comparisons and flagged risks, ready for CFO/board review.
